text.title: Symbol Index as Always-Fresh LSP/ctags Artifact Core Rules
text.summary: ≥5 testable rules pinning the methodology's preconditions, output shape, and skip path. Each rule pairs a binary check with rationale + named source.

rule.statement: Symbol index lives in the repo at a stable path (.symbol-index/ or tags) and is committed.
rule.rationale: Committed index means every clone is immediately usable; agents avoid expensive on-first-use builds.

rule.statement: Pre-commit hook regenerates the index on changed files; full rebuild only on scheduled CI.
rule.rationale: Incremental refresh keeps the index fresh on every commit without slowing the developer.

rule.statement: CI fails the build if the committed index does not match a fresh regeneration.
rule.rationale: Without the gate the committed index drifts and the agent reads stale data.

rule.statement: Use an LSP-based tool (Serena, SourceGraph SCIP) OR universal-ctags. Hand-rolled grep-indexers are forbidden.
rule.rationale: Hand-rolled tools miss inheritance, generics, macros; LSP/ctags handle them.

rule.statement: Agent queries the index via Bash invocation returning JSON (symbol → file:line, kind, refs), never via Read of binary blobs.
rule.rationale: JSON output is parseable; binary indexes are opaque to LLMs.

rule.statement: If repo < 5 KLOC OR LSP/ctags unavailable for the language OR full rebuild > 60s and incremental broken, STOP and use plain grep.
rule.rationale: On small or unsupported repos the index costs more than it returns.
